sentence: וַיֹּאמֶר אֱלֹהִים יִשְׁרְצוּ הַמַּיִם שֶׁרֶץ נֶפֶשׁ חַיָּה וְעוֹף יְעוֹפֵף עַל־הָאָרֶץ עַל־פְּנֵי רְקִיעַ הַשָּׁמָיִם

grammatical-notes.יְעוֹפֵף: Verb, Polel Imperfect 3ms. From 'uph' (to fly). The Polel stem indicates intense or repetitive flight.

dictionary-meaning.יִשְׁרְצוּ: Swarm with an abundance of swarming life — disclosing the repetitive 'verb-noun' pairing showing the intense density of life (Rule 21).
dictionary-meaning.נֶפֶשׁ חַיָּה: Living and breathing creatures — disclosing the 'Nephesh' as a biological, breathing entity (Rule 21).
dictionary-meaning.יְעוֹפֵף: Fly with intense and repetitive flight — disclosing the Polel stem's intensive force (Rule 9).

שרץ.chosen: swarm with an abundance of swarming life
שרץ.reason: Captures the dense, moving population of the waters intended by the author.

main-translation: ***Then Elohim decreed***, '***Let the waters swarm with an abundance of swarming life—living and breathing creatures; and let flying creatures fly with intense and repetitive flight above the ground, across the very face of the hammered-out expanse of the skies.***'

main-translation-explanation.swarm with an abundance of swarming life: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Disclosed the 'Sharatz' repetition which indicates a massive density of life (Rule 21).
main-translation-explanation.living and breathing creatures: Disclosed 'Nephesh Chayah' as a biological, breathing entity (Rule 21).
main-translation-explanation.fly with intense and repetitive flight: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Disclosed the Polel stem of 'uph' showing the active, soaring nature of the flight (Rule 9).

rejected-translations.reason: Thin; fails to disclose the specific 'swarming' and 'breathing' nature of the life.
